rag_rules: Preserve the AADL architecture by inserting AGREE annex blocks only in valid component type or implementation scopes
Place assumptions and guarantees in component type annexes when they reference component ports
Place implementation-level bindings in implementation annexes after subcomponents and connections
Use only visible identifiers from the current scope in AGREE annex blocks
generic_rules: Maintain the authoritative source of component structure from the input AADL model
Do not use subcomponent ports in component type contracts unless represented through valid variables or outputs
Preserve the original AADL property associations and do not encode them as AGREE expressions
Align helper declaration types with the actual expression types to avoid type mismatches
syntax_or_pattern_reminders: AGREE contracts are written inside `annex agree {** ... **};` blocks
Use exact Boolean expressions only when the port is an AGREE bool in scope
For output dependency contracts, relate visible outputs to visible inputs in component type annexes
anti_patterns: Do not remove generated clauses based on validator diagnostics without explicit guidance
Do not convert Boolean domain values into integers for range comparisons
Do not encode AADL timing properties as AGREE expressions unless explicitly exposed as ports or constants
irrelevant_topics: AGREE, annex, assume, guarantee, assign, validator, or repair
